title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: How to Make Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken recipe
authentic Korean Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken — a beloved Korean classic

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring crispy fried chicken coated in a sticky, sweet, and spicy sauce. This popular Korean street food is renowned for its irresistible crunch and perfectly balanced flavors. It is a fantastic meal for gatherings or a satisfying weeknight treat.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 1 teaspoon of salt, 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper, 1 tablespoon of ginger juice, and 1 tablespoon of soy sauce. Mix well to ensure the chicken is evenly coated. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: While the chicken marinates, prepare the batter. In a separate bowl, whisk together 1 cup of all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup of cornstarch, 1 teaspoon of baking powder, and 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Gradually add 1 cup of cold water (or sparkling water) to the dry ingredients, mixing until a smooth, pancake-like batter forms. Do not overmix.

instructions.title: First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat vegetable or canola oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 350F (175C). Dip each marinated chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it is fully coated, and carefully lower it into the hot oil. Fry the chicken in batches for 6-8 minutes, or until it is lightly golden brown and cooked through. Remove the chicken and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is frying, prepare the sauce. In a small saucepan, combine 3 tablespoons of gochujang, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, 2 tablespoons of rice vinegar, 2 tablespoons of honey or corn syrup, 1 tablespoon of brown sugar, 1 tablespoon of minced garlic, 1 teaspoon of grated fresh ginger, 1 teaspoon of sesame oil, and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from heat.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Crispy Perfection
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 375F (190C). Return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil in batches. Fry for another 2-3 minutes, or until the chicken is deeply golden brown and extra crispy. This double-frying method is key to achieving the signature Korean fried chicken crunch. Remove and drain on a wire rack.

instructions.title: Coat the Fried Chicken
instructions.text: Once all the chicken is double-fried and drained, transfer it to a large mixing bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken. Gently toss the chicken using tongs or a spatula until each piece is thoroughly coated with the sticky, flavorful sauce.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the sauced Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken to a serving platter. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and chopped scallions, if desired. Serve immediately while hot and crispy for the best experience. Enjoy with pickled radishes or a side of rice.

tips: For extra crispy chicken, use sparkling water in your batter. The carbonation creates a lighter, airier crust that holds up well to the double-frying process.
Adjust the spiciness of the Yangnyeom sauce by increasing or decreasing the amount of gochujang. For a sweeter sauce, add more honey or corn syrup to taste.
Serve immediately after coating for the best texture. Korean fried chicken is best enjoyed fresh. Pair with pickled daikon radish (mu-saengchae) to cut through the richness.
article:
Get ready to embark on a culinary journey that will tantalize your taste buds and transport you straight to the bustling streets of Seoul. Korean Fried Chicken, affectionately known as KFC (no, not that KFC!), has taken the world by storm, and at the heart of its global appeal lies a sticky, sweet, and spicy sensation: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken. This isn't just fried chicken; it's a masterpiece of texture and flavor, celebrated for its impossibly crispy exterior coated in a glossy, irresistible sauce. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that authentic Korean chicken experience at home, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through every step to master the art of making delicious Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken, a true gem among Korean chicken recipes.

## What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?

"Yangnyeom" literally translates to "seasoned sauce" in Korean, and when applied to chicken, it refers to a specific, iconic sweet and spicy glaze. Unlike its American counterpart, which often relies on a thick, bready coating, traditional Korean Fried Chicken is characterized by an ultra-thin, almost translucent batter that achieves an unparalleled crispness through a double-frying method. The magic truly happens when this perfectly fried chicken is tossed in the signature Yangnyeom sauce. This sauce is a symphony of flavors: the heat of gochujang (Korean chili paste) and gochugaru (Korean chili flakes), balanced by the sweetness of sugar or corn syrup, the savory depth of soy sauce, and a tangy kick from vinegar, often rounded out with garlic, ginger, and sesame oil. The result is a vibrant, glossy coating that clings to every crevice, delivering a harmonious blend of sweet, spicy, savory, and tangy notes with every satisfying crunch. It’s a dish that perfectly encapsulates the dynamic and bold flavors of Korean cuisine.

## Ingredients You Need

To create authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken, you'll need a few key ingredients, some of which are staples in Korean pantries.

**For the Chicken:**
*   **Chicken:** 2-3 lbs chicken wings or boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces. Wings are traditional, but thighs offer juicy, easy-to-eat bites.
*   **Marinade (optional but recommended):** 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp grated fresh ginger, 1 tsp minced garlic, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp black pepper.
*   **Dredging/Batter:** 1 cup all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup potato starch (or cornstarch), 1 tsp baking powder, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp black pepper, 1 cup cold water (or sparkling water for extra crispness), 1 egg (optional, for richer batter).
*   **Frying Oil:** 4-6 cups neutral oil with a high smoke point (canola, vegetable, peanut).

**For the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
*   **Gochujang:** 3 tbsp Korean chili paste (essential for authentic flavor).
*   **Gochugaru:** 1-2 tbsp Korean chili flakes (adjust to your spice preference).
*   **Soy Sauce:** 2 tbsp.
*   **Sugar/Syrup:** 2 tbsp brown sugar or corn syrup/rice syrup (for sweetness and gloss).
*   **Mirin:** 1 tbsp (Korean rice wine, for subtle sweetness and aroma).
*   **Vinegar:** 1 tbsp rice vinegar (for tang).
*   **Garlic:** 2-3 cloves, minced.
*   **Ginger:** 1 tsp, grated fresh.
*   **Sesame Oil:** 1 tsp.
*   **Water or Chicken Broth:** 2-3 tbsp (to thin the sauce to desired consistency).

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps carefully to achieve crispy, saucy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken perfection.

**1. Prepare the Chicken:**
   *   Pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. This is crucial for crispiness.
   *   If marinating, combine chicken with soy sauce, ginger, garlic, salt, and pepper. Let sit for at least 30 minutes in the refrigerator, or up to 2 hours.

**2. Make the Batter:**
   *   In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, potato starch, baking powder, salt, and pepper.
   *   Gradually whisk in the cold water and egg (if using) until you have a thin, pancake-like batter. It should be just thick enough to coat the chicken lightly.
   *   Add the marinated chicken to the batter, tossing to ensure each piece is evenly coated.

**3. First Fry (Cooking the Chicken):**
   *   Heat the frying oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 325-350°F (160-175°C). Use a thermometer for accuracy.
   *   Carefully add chicken pieces, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry in batches.
   *   Fry for 6-8 minutes, or until cooked through and lightly golden. The internal temperature should reach 165°F (74°C).
   *   Remove chicken with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain. Let it rest for 5-10 minutes.

**4. Second Fry (Achieving Maximum Crispness):**
   *   Increase the oil temperature to 350-375°F (175-190°C).
   *   Return the chicken to the hot oil for a second fry. Fry for another 2-4 minutes, or until deeply golden brown and audibly crispy. This double-frying technique is key to that signature Korean crunch.
   *   Remove chicken and place it back on the wire rack to drain any excess oil.

**5. Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
   *   While the chicken is frying, combine all Yangnyeom sauce ingredients (gochujang, gochugaru, soy sauce, sugar/syrup, mirin, rice vinegar, minced garlic, grated ginger, sesame oil, and water/broth) in a medium saucepan.
   *   Bring to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens to a glossy, syrupy consistency, about 5-7 minutes. Taste and adjust sweetness or spice as desired.

**6. Coat the Chicken:**
   *   Immediately after the second fry, transfer the hot, crispy chicken to a large bowl.
   *   Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken.
   *   Using tongs or two spatulas, gently toss the chicken until every piece is evenly coated in the sticky, delicious sauce.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

*   **Double Fry is Non-Negotiable:** This is the secret to the incredibly crispy, light crust that Korean Fried Chicken is famous for. Don't skip it!
*   **Oil Temperature Control:** Use a deep-fry thermometer. Maintaining the correct oil temperature prevents soggy chicken or chicken that burns before it cooks through.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pot:** Frying in small batches ensures the oil temperature doesn't drop too much, leading to crispier chicken.
*   **Pat Chicken Dry:** Excess moisture on the chicken skin will prevent the batter from adhering properly and result in a less crispy crust.
*   **Taste and Adjust Sauce:** The Yangnyeom sauce can be customized. Add more gochugaru for extra heat, more sugar for sweetness, or a squeeze of lemon for brightness.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is best enjoyed fresh off the stove, when the chicken is at its crispiest and the sauce is perfectly sticky.

## Serving Suggestions

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a star on its own, but it pairs wonderfully with a few traditional Korean sides.
*   **Pickled Radish (Mu Sam):** The refreshing tang and crunch of pickled radish is the perfect counterpoint to the rich, spicy chicken.
*   **Coleslaw:** A simple, creamy coleslaw provides a cool, crisp texture contrast.
*   **Beer (Chimaek):** In Korea, fried chicken and beer ("chimaek") is a beloved pairing. A cold lager or light ale cuts through the richness beautifully.
*   **Rice:** Serve with a side of steamed white rice for a more substantial meal.
*   **Garnish:** A sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds and thinly sliced green onions adds visual appeal and a nutty aroma.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is divine, you can experiment with variations to suit your taste or dietary needs.
*   **Spice Level:** Easily adjust the amount of gochujang and gochugaru in the sauce to make it milder or fiery hot.
*   **Sweetness:** Experiment with different sweeteners like honey, maple syrup, or even apple juice concentrate for a unique flavor profile.
*   **Chicken Parts:** While wings and thighs are popular, you can use boneless chicken breasts cut into chunks for a leaner, quicker-cooking option. Just be mindful not to overcook them.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Use tamari or gluten-free soy sauce and a gluten-free flour blend or solely potato starch for the batter.
*   **Healthier Twist:** For a less indulgent version, you can bake or air-fry the chicken until crispy, then toss it in the Yangnyeom sauce. While it won't replicate the deep-fried crispness exactly, it's a delicious alternative.
*   **Nutty Flavor:** For an added layer of richness and texture, stir a tablespoon of smooth peanut butter into the sauce.

Mastering Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken at home is an incredibly rewarding experience. With its irresistible sweet and spicy glaze and unparalleled crunch, this dish is more than just a meal – it's a celebration of flavor and texture that will impress your family and friends. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and get ready to enjoy one of the most beloved Korean chicken recipes right in your own kitchen!
